subject: Cloud Computing
title: Sovereign Clouds and Data Nationalism
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Sovereign Clouds and Data Nationalism.

hook.text: Countries are demanding that their citizens' data stays within their borders. Sovereign clouds are the answer.

buildup.text: GDPR in Europe, data localization laws in India and China — governments want control over where data physically resides.

discovery.text: Cloud providers are building sovereign regions: isolated infrastructure operated by local partners under local laws.

twist.text: Sovereign clouds fragment the global internet. What was one seamless cloud becomes a patchwork of national boundaries.

climax.text: Global innovation versus national sovereignty — that tension is reshaping how cloud providers build their services.

punchline.text: Your data must stay in your country. The cloud obeys borders now.

quiz.question: What drives the demand for sovereign clouds?
options: Data localization laws that require citizens' data to stay within national borders
Cloud providers wanting to charge more for regional services
Technical limitations that prevent data from crossing borders
